title: Asian Stocks Surge After Cease-Fire Announcement
summary: Asian stock markets are up across the board after President Trump’s announcement of a cease-fire with Iran.  Japan’s Nikkei Stock Average closed its Wednesday morning session up 5%, while South Korea’s Kospi index is up nearly 6% and the Hang Seng Index in Hong Kong is up 2.5%.  The oil-price move is a relief for major oil importers such as Japan and South Korea.

title: Versamet Gold Stream Reshapes Skeena Eskay Creek Funding And Cash Flows
summary: Versamet Royalties has entered a definitive agreement to acquire a 3.52% gold stream on Skeena Resources' Eskay Creek project. The stream is tied directly to gold production from Eskay Creek, Skeena's flagship asset, and adjusts the royalty and streaming profile on the project. This transaction follows earlier focus on debt financing and a stream buy down, and introduces a new third party participant to Eskay Creek's future cash flows. title: Oil prices drop and stocks rally after Trump’s ceasefire announcement
summary: Oil prices plummeted Tuesday after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, spurring hopes that more oil tankers would be able to transit the key Strait of Hormuz soon.

title: Agnico Eagle Cascadia Alliance Adds Long-Term Gold Copper Exploration Potential
summary: Agnico Eagle Mines (NYSE:AEM) has entered a multi year alliance with Cascadia Minerals to explore for gold and copper in Yukon's Stikine Terrane. The agreement focuses on a highly prospective, underexplored region with funding commitments from Agnico Eagle for exploration programs. The arrangement includes options for Agnico Eagle to earn a majority interest in new discoveries and potentially form joint ventures with Cascadia. title: Lundin Mining Completes Acquisition of an Additional 5% Ownership in Caserones and a 31% Interest in the Los Helados Project

title: Asian Stock Markets Are Also Soaring
summary: President Trump's social-media post kicked off a global rally, with Japan's Nikkei recently up 4.4% and South Korea's Kospi adding around 5%. International stocks have been particularly hard hit by the Iran war, with investors fearing the recent surge in energy prices would slow growth around the world.

title: ‘Freak Out’ Indicator Soars to Record With War Sparking Trader Anxiety
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Investors wary of being wrong-footed by twists and turns in the Iran war are trading stocks at a record intensity, one market measure shows. Wall Street traders have been on edge, hanging on every development in the Middle East and the often unpredictable missives of President Donald Trump. One technical metric, the daily turnover in the State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, has breached $60 billion — a reading seen as a “freak out” indicator by Bloomberg Intelligence strategists —

title: Gold and Silver Prices Pop Higher
summary: Gold futures are on track for one of their best days this year, rebounding from recent lows after President Trump said he'd agreed to a two-week cease fire with Iran.  Most-active gold futures traded about 4% higher late Tuesday in New York, adding more than $150 a troy ounce.

title: Gold Climbs After Trump Agrees to a Two-Week Ceasefire in Iran
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Gold advanced after US President Donald Trump and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire to finalize talks on ending the war that’s upended global markets.Bullion climbed as much as 3.1% to above $4,850 an ounce, extending a 1.2% gain in the previous session. title: Precious Metals Rise, Boosted by Dollar Weakness, Lower Treasury Yields
summary: Precious metals rose in early trade, boosted by dollar weakness which makes USD-denominated gold and silver cheaper for holders of non-USD currencies.

title: Stocks Move Even Higher After Iran Agrees to Open the Strait
summary: Stocks were already up about 2% in after-hours trading when Trump announced a two-week ceasefire, if Iran reopened the strait. They jumped even higher when Iran agreed. S&P 500 futures gained 2.3% on the news.

title: Stocks Jump, Oil Prices Drop on Trump's Announcement
summary: S&P 500 futures climbed by 2% and futures tracking Brent crude fell by 5.4% to $103.42 a barrel after President Trump announced an extension of Iran's deadline by two weeks.  The president noted that the extension is “subject to the Islamic Republic of Iran agreeing to the COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING of the Strait of Hormuz Iran.”
